lines: Celia Knotwell, road courier, currently outpaced by sand and bad manners.
Carry this ribbon-word to the castle scribe: the Sunken Road is shedding its old stones into the wash.
The shade is holding, the jars are full, and your feet are quicker than my cart. That is enough good news for one road.
Mind the ribbon cairn, traveler. It marks where the road still remembers being dry.
